tagline: Patches, configures and provisions across the client fleet — staged and reversible, with a human on high-blast-radius changes.
problem: Managed-IT work (patching, config, access) is labour-heavy across many clients; one bad autonomous change is a multi-tenant outage or a privilege-escalation incident.
outcome: Changes staged through rings with health gates and auto-rollback; JIT least-privilege access; tenant isolation; fleet-wide / privileged / destructive changes escalated.

steps.does: Detect the change needed (patch / config / remediation) and take a pre-change snapshot
Plan a staged rollout (rings + health gates + tested rollback) scoped to one tenant
Check blast radius, JIT least-privilege, tenant isolation and SOC 2 controls
A change manager approves fleet-wide, privileged or destructive changes
Apply to the canary ring with a health gate; auto-halt and roll back on regression
Verify, widen the rollout, and log every action for the SOC 2 audit trail
